skill.name: web-cli-teleport
skill.description: Help users choose Claude Code Web, CLI, or a hybrid workflow for task planning and session handoff.
skill.summary: Plan when to use Claude Code Web, CLI, or teleport handoff workflows.
skill.author: DNYoussef

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high-risk patterns, but manual review found no executable code, network calls, credential access, or prompt injection. The Ruby backtick, weak cryptography, and reconnaissance detections are false positives caused by Markdown examples, Claude Code wording, and workflow labels. The only retained risk factor is low-risk guidance that asks users to run an official Claude CLI teleport command.

low_findings.title: User-Run CLI Teleport Command
low_findings.description: The skill instructs users to copy and run a Claude CLI teleport command. This is legitimate workflow guidance, but terminal commands should be verified before use because they can affect local sessions and context transfer.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command is directly documented and user executed, with no hidden script or dynamic interpolation. The risk is limited because it uses the official Claude CLI syntax and does not include destructive flags.

low_findings.title: Static Pattern Matches Are Markdown Documentation
low_findings.description: The reported Ruby backtick, weak cryptography, system reconnaissance, and network reconnaissance findings appear in prose, fenced examples, and workflow labels. No evidence found of executable Ruby, cryptographic implementation, scanning logic, or unauthorized network behavior.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review confirms these lines are Markdown documentation and YAML-shaped examples only. The repository contains SKILL.md and a Graphviz process diagram, with no executable source file implementing the flagged behavior.

content.value_statement: Teams often lose time choosing the wrong Claude Code interface for a task. This skill gives clear criteria for using Web, CLI, or a hybrid teleport workflow.
actual_capabilities: Compares Claude Code Web and CLI for different task types.
Recommends Web, CLI, or hybrid workflows based on complexity and testing needs.
Explains when to teleport a session from Web to CLI.
Lists setup steps for preserving context during handoff.
Provides failure modes and mitigations for dirty git state or large context.
limitations: Does not execute teleport commands or modify local repositories.
Assumes the user has access to Claude Code Web and Claude CLI.
Does not validate whether a specific session identifier is valid.
Does not replace local security review before running terminal commands.

use_cases.title: Plan Remote Fixes
use_cases.description: Decide whether a simple repository change can be handled from Claude Code Web while away from a workstation.
use_cases.target_user: Developer on mobile or shared computer

use_cases.title: Move Complex Work Local
use_cases.description: Identify when a task needs local tests, debugging, or file exploration and should move from Web to CLI.

use_cases.title: Coordinate Review Flow
use_cases.description: Use Web for review and approval while keeping CLI available for deeper revisions and local validation.

prompt_templates.title: Choose an Interface
prompt_templates.prompt: I need to complete this task: [describe task]. Should I use Claude Code Web, Claude CLI, or a hybrid workflow?

prompt_templates.title: Plan a Hybrid Workflow
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me plan a Web to CLI workflow for this task: [describe task]. Include likely teleport points and review steps.

prompt_templates.title: Assess a Mid-Task Switch
prompt_templates.scenario: Existing Web session is becoming complex
prompt_templates.prompt: I started this in Claude Code Web, but now I need testing and debugging. Decide whether I should teleport to CLI and list the handoff steps.

prompt_templates.title: Design a Team Review Process
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a team workflow that combines Claude Code Web review, CLI implementation, teleport handoff, local testing, and final pull request approval.

output_examples.input: I need to update a small translation file while traveling.
output_examples.output: Recommendation: Use Claude Code Web. The task is small, does not require local services, and can be reviewed as a pull request.

output_examples.input: I need to refactor authentication and run local integration tests.
output_examples.output: Recommendation: Use Claude CLI. The task needs repeated edits, local test execution, and close review of file changes.

output_examples.input: I began a styling fix on Web, but it now affects many components.
output_examples.output: Recommendation: Switch to a hybrid workflow. Finish the current checkpoint, prepare a clean local git state, then continue in CLI.

best_practices: Use Web for small, well-defined tasks that do not need local services.
Use CLI for debugging, broad refactors, and work that needs local tests.
Verify git state and command text before teleporting a session.
anti_patterns: Do not force complex debugging into Web when local tools are required.
Do not teleport with uncommitted local changes that could confuse the handoff.
Do not run copied terminal commands before checking the source and expected effect.

faq.question: Does this skill run the teleport command for me?
faq.answer: No. It explains when and how to use teleport workflows, but the user runs any CLI command manually.

faq.question: When should I choose Claude Code Web?
faq.answer: Choose Web for small tasks, mobile access, quick pull requests, and work that does not need local tools.

faq.question: When should I choose Claude CLI?
faq.answer: Choose CLI for complex implementation, debugging, broad file exploration, local services, and repeated test runs.

faq.question: What does teleporting preserve?
faq.answer: The guide states that teleporting preserves session context and conversation history for continuation in another interface.

faq.question: What should I check before teleporting?
faq.answer: Check that your local git state is clean and that the copied command matches the expected Claude CLI command.

faq.question: Can this skill validate my local environment?
faq.answer: No. It can recommend a workflow, but it does not inspect installed tools, credentials, or repository health.
